jokes.text: Another big night, fellas... $164.87.
jokes.setup: Mac announces it was a 'big night' at the bar
jokes.punchline: The pathetic amount of $164.87 reveals their failure
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic setup/punchline structure establishing their pathetic situation with economy

jokes.text: A week ago was three weeks late, Mac.
jokes.setup: Mac thinks they paid mortgage on time
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals their 'on time' payment was actually three weeks late
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing their delusional incompetence and warped perspective on reality

jokes.text: So could you guys try to not be weird?
jokes.setup: Dee asks them to act normal for her friend
jokes.punchline: Implies they're normally weird, which they immediately prove
jokes.explanation: Sets up the gang's inability to act normal, pays off immediately

jokes.text: We're cool! / Not cool? / Try to be cool. / Sometimes you're not too cool. / Always cool, man.
jokes.setup: They insist they're cool
jokes.punchline: Their desperate overlapping protests prove they're not cool
jokes.explanation: Multiple characters proving Dee's point through their very denials

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa! We don't want any trouble.
jokes.setup: Black man enters the bar
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately assumes he's there for trouble, revealing racist assumptions
jokes.explanation: Instantly reveals Mac's racist assumptions in a perfectly awkward way

jokes.text: Guess you guys don't have too many brothers walking through here.
jokes.setup: Terrell observes their reaction
jokes.punchline: Calls out the obvious racial dynamic at play
jokes.explanation: Sets up the racial tension that drives the episode

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. We got plenty of brothers. Yeah... A-African-Americans.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to deny racial exclusion
jokes.punchline: His awkward correction to 'African-Americans' makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Mac's attempt at political correctness backfiring

jokes.text: Yeah. We're probably related.
jokes.setup: Mac suggesting Terrell might know their black customers
jokes.punchline: Assumes all black people are related to each other
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures casual racism with devastating economy

jokes.text: We weren't expecting you to be black, that's all.
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to explain their reaction
jokes.punchline: States their racist assumption directly and matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Dennis's matter-of-fact racism delivered with complete obliviousness

jokes.text: Thanks, guys.
jokes.setup: The gang has thoroughly embarrassed themselves
jokes.punchline: Dee's sarcastic thanks for proving her point about being weird
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to Dee's request to not be weird, which they spectacularly failed

jokes.text: But, just to be clear, when you say your sister, do you mean your sister or your friend...
jokes.setup: Terrell mentions his sister
jokes.punchline: Mac assumes black people call all women 'sister'
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's continued racial assumptions and stereotyping

jokes.text: I mean my sister.
jokes.setup: Mac's racist assumption about terminology
jokes.punchline: Terrell's patient clarification that he means exactly what he said
jokes.explanation: Terrell's patience in the face of casual racism is both funny and telling

jokes.text: Yo, ese... unless you want to get yo' ass torn apart... you'd better get that look off your face.
jokes.setup: Terrell tells story about confronting someone
jokes.punchline: Uses stereotypical tough guy language that builds tension
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation of violent confrontation with movie-like dialogue

jokes.text: I guess you gon' have to tear my ass apart, homes.
jokes.setup: Story builds to violent confrontation
jokes.punchline: The 'threat' uses sexual innuendo that completely changes meaning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that reframes the entire story as sexual, not violent

jokes.text: Took him to the back alley... and I tore his ass apart!
jokes.setup: Everyone expects he beat up the guy
jokes.punchline: Uses same language but it's clearly about sex now
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the misdirection, completely recontextualizes the story

jokes.text: Oh, bro, that's racist. / No, asshole, that's not what I meant.
jokes.setup: Mac says something about Terrell being 'off'
jokes.punchline: Dennis calls him racist while Mac gets defensive
jokes.explanation: Shows their awareness of racism while still being racist

jokes.text: That's true. They do have 'niggers hanging from rafters.'
jokes.setup: Mac trying to quote Terrell to support hiring him
jokes.punchline: Uses the racial slur completely inappropriately in front of the waitress
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe as Mac completely misreads when it's appropriate to quote someone

jokes.text: Hitler? / No, I'm not... I'm not Adolf Hitler.
jokes.setup: Mac's racist comment
jokes.punchline: Waitress sarcastically calls him Hitler, Mac takes it literally
jokes.explanation: Mac's literal response to obvious sarcasm shows his complete obliviousness

jokes.text: I'll make sure to put lots of cream in yours.
jokes.setup: Mac's racist comment about black people
jokes.punchline: Waitress sarcastically offers to make his coffee white
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subtle dig at Mac's racism using coffee as metaphor

jokes.text: 'Bout to bust that shit up, Reese. 'Bout to bust that shit up, boy!
jokes.setup: Mac tries to talk to black students to make friends
jokes.punchline: Uses completely inappropriate slang and wrong names
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt at 'black' slang fails spectacularly and awkwardly

jokes.text: You're trying to make black friends. - You're not making it easy. - You're trying to impress Terrell with a couple of black friends.
jokes.setup: Dennis wants to diversify their friend group
jokes.punchline: Mac correctly identifies Dennis's superficial motivation
jokes.explanation: Mac sees through Dennis's performative wokeness, showing their friendship dynamics

jokes.text: Domino, bitch!
jokes.setup: Charlie claims he's not good at dominoes
jokes.punchline: He's actually excellent and celebrates inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation and shows Charlie's hidden competitiveness

jokes.text: Maybe it's comments like that. / I meant those people in there, not all black people.
jokes.setup: Mac complains about 'these people' liking Charlie more
jokes.punchline: His clarification still sounds racist
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt to clarify only makes his racism more obvious

jokes.text: Physical fight over wallet with photo
jokes.setup: Mac tries to get Dennis's wallet to prove his obsession
jokes.punchline: They have an embarrassing public fight revealing the photo
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that reveals Dennis's creepy obsession publicly

jokes.text: Just give me it. I'll call the black girl. Okay?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to end the fight
jokes.punchline: Reduces the woman to just 'the black girl'
jokes.explanation: Dennis's reductive language shows he still doesn't understand why his behavior is problematic

jokes.text: I hate listening to people's dreams. It's like flipping through a stack of photographs. If I'm not in any of'em and nobody's having sex, I just... I don't care.
jokes.setup: Dee starts telling about her dream
jokes.punchline: Dennis's narcissistic take on why dreams are boring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism wrapped in a relatable observation about dreams

jokes.text: Gentlemen, I understand my services have been requested.
jokes.setup: Customer approaches the group at the bar
jokes.punchline: Reveals the bar has accidentally become a gay hookup spot
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist revealing what kind of bar Terrell actually created

jokes.text: You have the most beautiful eyes. So blue. Really more of a blue-green, actually, but...
jokes.setup: Gay customer hits on Mac
jokes.punchline: Mac gets flustered but accidentally engages with the compliment
jokes.explanation: Mac's vanity conflicts with his homophobia in a perfectly uncomfortable way

jokes.text: You guys are running the hottest gay bar in Philadelphia.
jokes.setup: They think they have a successful regular bar
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually known as a gay bar
jokes.explanation: Major revelation that recontextualizes their 'success' entirely

jokes.text: 'Looking for that new hot spot to spot that stud? Well, Paddy's Irish Pub has plugged that hole.'
jokes.setup: Dennis reads a review of their bar
jokes.punchline: The sexual innuendo in the gay bar review
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels with gay bar context

jokes.text: Business ethics? The only reason you don't care is you have black girls hanging all over you.
jokes.setup: Mac claims to have business ethics about not running a gay bar
jokes.punchline: Reveals his real motivation is jealousy over Charlie's success with women
jokes.explanation: Exposes Mac's hypocrisy and real motivations behind his moral stance

jokes.text: No, you're leading them on. You're not gay, you're just really vain.
jokes.setup: Dennis claims he gets along well with gay customers
jokes.punchline: Mac identifies that Dennis enjoys the attention for narcissistic reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight that explains Dennis's behavior with surgical precision

jokes.text: 'What do you want, Charlie?' I'd like you to take a look at the lovely young African-American woman... sitting at the table over there.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to order coffee and prove he's not racist
jokes.punchline: His awkward, forced attempt to showcase his black friend
jokes.explanation: Mac's transparent attempt to use Janell as a prop is painfully obvious. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Charlie's using you to prove that he's not racist. Then he asked me out on a date.
jokes.setup: Charlie brings Janell to the coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Janell exposes Charlie's transparent and offensive scheme
jokes.explanation: Brutally exposes Charlie's manipulative and racist behavior

jokes.text: He said that Janell punched me in my eye! She's got a hell of a right hand. She also said if she ever saw me again, she was going to slit my throat.
jokes.setup: Charlie's black eye needs explanation
jokes.punchline: Reveals Janell's violent reaction and ongoing threat
jokes.explanation: Shows the escalating consequences of Charlie's racist behavior

jokes.text: It's not that they like us. It's that they don't like you. You know why? Uh, because you're an asshole!
jokes.setup: Mac wonders why people like the others but not him
jokes.punchline: Janell directly calls out that Mac is simply an asshole
jokes.explanation: Simple, direct character assessment that cuts through Mac's self-delusion

jokes.text: It's not that they like us. It's that they don't like you. You know why? Uh, because you're an asshole!
jokes.setup: Mac wondering why everyone likes Charlie and Dennis more
jokes.punchline: Charlie's blunt assessment of Mac's personality
jokes.explanation: Charlie's rare moment of brutal honesty cuts through Mac's delusions

jokes.text: We gotta start with Dennis. Can you get him so drunk tonight? On tequila. But a lot of it. Enough that maybe he might hurt himself.
jokes.setup: Dee has a plan to solve their problems
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests getting Dennis dangerously drunk
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Dee's willingness to potentially harm her brother for a scheme

jokes.text: What the f... What does that mean? Did we have... No. No. Don't be silly. It was all hands.
jokes.setup: Dennis wakes up confused after being drunk
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had a sexual encounter but 'only' manual contact
jokes.explanation: Reveals the consequences of Dennis's vanity and Mac's manipulation

jokes.text: How's that ass feelin'?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to understand what happened
jokes.punchline: Implication that more happened than Dennis realizes
jokes.explanation: Suggests Dennis experienced more than he remembers or admits

jokes.text: Oh. You had an experiment, and you want to go back to the way it was before the experiment. - Interesting. - The bar. We had our experiment with the bar.
jokes.setup: Dennis wants to stop being a gay bar
jokes.punchline: Charlie's word choice implies Dennis experimented sexually
jokes.explanation: Charlie's deliberate ambiguity about what kind of 'experimenting' Dennis did

jokes.text: Yeah! That's the crazy bitch that punched me in my eye! - Charlie! That's my sister.
jokes.setup: Charlie recognizes the woman who punched him
jokes.punchline: She's Terrell's actual sister
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that proves Mac's racist assumption was wrong while creating new conflict

jokes.text: And then it turns out that you people actually are! No, that's not what I meant. Hold on a second. I meant that you two actually are.
jokes.setup: Mac learns Terrell and Janell are actually siblings
jokes.punchline: His attempt to clarify his racism only makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Mac's earlier racist assumption, making it even more cringe

jokes.text: Because earlier you were implying that I was racist... because you thought that I was implying that all black people are related. And then it turns out that you people actually are!
jokes.setup: Mac trying to defend himself about the sister confusion
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by saying 'you people'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where Mac's attempt to prove he's not racist makes him sound more racist

jokes.text: How's that ass feelin'?
jokes.setup: Dee asks why Dennis was so freaked out if her plan didn't work
jokes.punchline: Charlie repeats the gay customer's line, revealing what actually happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reveals the real reason for Dennis's trauma
